title: Do not enforce http(s)/ws(s) schema for router url
author: KSDaemon
body:
Since Wampy allows passing a custom transport implementation via a WebSocket-like interface, the actual transport is not strictly required to be WebSocket-compatible. In practice, it can be anything — WebSocket, long-polling, or any other protocol, as long as it satisfies the expected interface.

Because of this, enforcing WSS specifically may be too restrictive. We should instead allow passing any valid transport implementation that conforms to the required interface.

In particular, this means we should support custom transports (e.g., a custom WebSocket or non-WebSocket transport) without assuming or enforcing WSS semantics at this level.

title: `onConnect` callback never triggered
author: na2axl
body:
**Describe the bug**

The `onConnect` callback in the options is never triggered.

**To Reproduce**

1. Create a Wampy instance
2. Add a callback for `onConnect` in the options
3. Connect to the WAMP router
4. The instance got connected, but the `onConnect` callback is not triggered

**Expected behavior**

The `onConnect` callback is called on every first connections, then the `onReconnect` callback is called on every subsequent reconnections.

**Environment:**
-  
 - OS: Linux 6.13.7-orbstack-00283-g9d1400e7e9c6 aarch64 (docker)
 - bun v1.2.8+adab0f64f
 - Wampy.js v7.1.1
 - WAMP Router: Nexus

**Additional context**

The issue is both in Bun and Node.js

title: Chore: Update chai to the latest
author: KSDaemon
body:
It's been some time since chai v5 release, and currently, tests are failing with this update. 
It will be good to find and fix the root clause.
This refs to https://github.com/KSDaemon/wampy.js/pull/442

title: Docs sometime reference the wampy instance as `wampy` and other times as `ws`
author: lucasrmendonca
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
We should probably reference the wampy instance in the docs in a consistent way to avoid confusion

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Renaming `ws` occurences as `wampy`

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Or renaming `wampy` occurences as `ws`

**Additional context**
None

title: End-2-End Encryption
author: KSDaemon
body: It would be cool to add an e2ee as all building blocks like ppt are already in place.

title: Ability to work with different crypto libs
author: KSDaemon
body:
wamp-cra & crytosign auth flow require some crypto algo provided by a few different libs. It would be cool to not hardly depend on them but instead provide a kind of interface and/or DI layer to be able to use different libs. 

The main reason for that is that big projects may already use some of the lib and that is not great to pull another crypto library to the project, taking into account that most of the time such libs are pretty big.

title: Merge "wampy-cryptosign" plugin into main codebase.
author: KSDaemon
body:
It will be easier to maintain the authorization plugins inside the main code repo rather than in a separate one. And I believe any production system will have the authentication enabled so it probably will use some auth plugins.

https://github.com/KSDaemon/wampy-cryptosign

title: Merge "wampy-cra" plugin into main codebase.
author: KSDaemon
body:
It will be easier to maintain the authorization plugins inside the main code repo rather than in a separate one. And I believe any production system will have the authentication enabled so it probably will use some auth plugins.

https://github.com/KSDaemon/wampy-cra

title: Unwrapping single result
author: KSDaemon
body:
Those familiar with Autobahn family WAMP clients — know that all Autobahn clients are unwrapping the single positional argument when returning the result to the client code. This means that whenever you receive from the wire WAMP messages like this: `[50, 7814135, {}, ["Hello, world!"]]` (it is a RESULT message), the client-side code will get the single string `"Hello, world!"` as the result instead of array `["Hello, world!"]`. This happens automatically whenever the payload consists only of a positional argument with one item (the type and structure of the item don't matter). 

While this behavior may be convenient when you work only inside Autobahn family stack, it leads to some complications when you have other WAMP clients running, because developers got confused about why in one case remote procedure returns `"Hello, world!"` and `["Hello, world!"]` in other. 

So it would be pretty neat to have a compatibility flag explicitly set to `ON` to unwrap such single results. 

This flag may be set on the instance level or granularly for a specific CALL/SUBSCRIBE.

title: Incorrect register options during automatic reregistration
author: KSDaemon
body:
Wampy is not preserving the original RPC registration options so in case of network failure and auto reconnection - no options are passed to the router.

Found by @PatrLind during investigation in #386

title: Unhandled Rejection at Promise
author: PatrLind
body:
**Describe the bug**
When reconnecting to the server I often get errors like: `register for already registered procedure 'TOPIC'`
When this happens the _wsOnMessage doesn't handle the error and create an unhandled rejection at promise error.

**Expected behavior**
The error should probably be handled by a try catch in _wsOnMessage(). 

**Stack trace**
```
RegisterError: Wamp error
    at Wampy._onErrorMessage (file:///PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/wampy/src/wampy.js:1078:39)
    at 8 (file:///PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/wampy/src/wampy.js:875:54)
    at Wampy._wsOnMessage (file:///PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/wampy/src/wampy.js:908:19)
    at WebSocket._ws.onmessage (file:///PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/wampy/src/wampy.js:777:46)
    at callListener (/PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/ws/lib/event-target.js:290:14)
    at WebSocket.onMessage (/PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/ws/lib/event-target.js:209:9)
    at WebSocket.emit (node:events:513:28)
    at WebSocket.emit (node:domain:489:12)
    at Receiver.receiverOnMessage (/PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/ws/lib/websocket.js:1209:20)
    at Receiver.emit (node:events:513:28)
    at Receiver.emit (node:domain:489:12)
    at /PROJECT_PATH/node_modules/ws/lib/receiver.js:608:16
    at node:internal/process/task_queues:141:7
    at AsyncResource.runInAsyncScope (node:async_hooks:203:9)
    at AsyncResource.runMicrotask (node:internal/process/task_queues:138:8)
    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:96:5)
```

title: Feature request: Event Retention
author: ialse
body:
I did not find an implementation of getting the last published message when subscribing to a topic. Has this been implemented or planned in future releases?

Read more here:
https://github.com/wamp-proto/wamp-proto/blob/da34d9bd833beeb6f9cc8bc89faf8138d710aa78/rfc/text/advanced/ap_pubsub_event_retention.md#event-retention

title: subscription key is "topic-undefined" when no options are passed
author: lucasrmendonca
body:
**Expected behavior**
Options should be included in the subscription key only if they are passed, if not, they should be omitted.

title: Network connection interruption not detected
author: Javierd
body:
**Describe the bug**
When the client is connected to a server and the client network fails (e.g., the data and wifi are disabled) the client never notices the problem, and when trying to execute a RPC call, the call never returns, and doesn't throw any errors neither.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Connect to a remote server
2. Disable the client's network connections
3. Note that the client never notices the problem
4. Try to execute a RPC call
5. Note that the client never notices the problem

**Expected behavior**
The component should note that the connection is lost, it should retry to connect and, if it can't, it should call the _onclose_ callback.

**Environment (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux
 - Browser and version: Chrome 109.0.5414.119
 - Version of Wampy.js library: 7.0.2
 - Wampcc master branch

title: disconnect() method never returning when onClose is set
author: Javierd

title: Crossbar tests are failing since #227
author: lucasrmendonca
body:
**Describe the bug**
Since the Mocha dependency bump from 9.2.2 to 10.2.0 (#227) my crossbar tests are failing

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. `git checkout origin/dev`
2. `npm install`
3. `npm run test:crossbar`

**Expected behavior**
Crossbar tests should pass

**Current behavior**
```
1) Wampy.js with Crossbar
       Works with Json serializer:
```
